summary: Task 6 (AboutScreen diagnostics section + 7-tap dev-gesture) audited against OWASP Top 10 with the focus areas from the task brief (A01 access control, A03 injection, A05 Hilt misconfiguration, A08 integrity / data leakage, A09 logging failures, intent/activity-result hygiene, path traversal). No vulnerabilities found. The 7-tap gesture cannot be triggered by a remote caller (requires seven local taps inside the app's own About screen, which is reached via in-app navigation from MainActivity; no exported intent-filter advertises a direct path to About). The gesture's RuntimeException escalates nothing: it flows into CrashHandler -> writes filesDir/logs/crash.log -> starts the non-exported CrashReportActivity in the :crash process -> Process.killProcess(myPid). No permissions, no foreign IPC targets, no files outside the app's private storage. The SAF suggested filename 'sanctum-log-${timestamp}.txt' interpolates only a SimpleDateFormat('yyyyMMdd-HHmm', Locale.ROOT) value from Date() — no user-controlled component. The returned content:// URI is consumed solely by ContentResolver.openOutputStream inside LogExportManager.writeTo; it is never converted to a filesystem path, concatenated, or logged. LogExportModule's @Binds for DefaultCommandRunner and AndroidDeviceInfoProvider are correctly installed in SingletonComponent (they feed the @Singleton-scoped LogExportManager); no activity-level leak. On IOException from writeTo, the exception is caught at the AboutViewModel boundary and surfaced to the UI only as the localized string R.string.log_export_error_toast — neither the exception message nor its stack trace reaches the Snackbar. The test-crash path adds no accidental logging (no Log.e / ErrorLog.e calls on the RuntimeException route — CrashHandler is the only writer of crash.log, as designed). Decision 11 (unfiltered .txt content on Phase 2.5) and Decision 12 (dev-gesture active in release) are explicitly in-scope project decisions — not flagged, per the task brief. ABOUT_ASSET='about.md' remains a hardcoded constant (D17 path-traversal protection preserved). No new attack surface, no new dependencies, no network or crypto touchpoints.

findings.description: Informational: @InstallIn(SingletonComponent::class) with @Binds for DefaultCommandRunner and AndroidDeviceInfoProvider is the correct scope. Both impls are stateless helpers (@Inject constructor() and @Inject constructor(@ApplicationContext Context) respectively) consumed by the @Singleton LogExportManager; they do not hold or leak Activity references. Matches the existing ImageDecoder module pattern. No issue — recorded for traceability.
findings.suggestion: No change needed. If future bindings in this module depend on activity-scoped state, consider splitting them into a separate ActivityRetainedComponent-installed module to preserve the principle-of-least-scope posture.

findings.description: Informational: the 7-tap gesture on the version line is active in both debug and release builds per Decision 12 (user-spec 'Ограничения'). Confirmed no privilege escalation: the RuntimeException on confirm runs on the UI thread, is captured by Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler -> CrashHandler, which writes to filesDir/logs/crash.log (app-private), launches the non-exported CrashReportActivity (android:exported="false", android:process=":crash"), then Process.killProcess(myPid). No new permissions, no file writes outside filesDir, no foreign intent targets. Remote triggering is impossible — the gesture requires seven local taps inside AboutScreen, which has no intent-filter and is reached only via in-app navigation from MainActivity.
findings.suggestion: No action for Phase 2.5. Phase 5 backlog entry (NOTES.md) already tracks wrapping the gesture in BuildConfig.DEBUG before first public release, which will further reduce residual exposure. Optional hardening today: gate the AlertDialog confirm behind a second verification (e.g., a typed phrase) only if user-spec tolerates friction — not recommended given Decision 12's explicit acceptance.

findings.description: Informational: the SAF suggested filename 'sanctum-log-${timestamp}.txt' uses SimpleDateFormat('yyyyMMdd-HHmm', Locale.ROOT).format(Date()). Locale.ROOT forces arabic digits and an invariant pattern; the Date() is process-local; the pattern itself contains only fixed characters. There is no user-controlled component anywhere in the filename. SAF treats this as a user-overridable suggestion and the OS document provider applies its own sanitization before creating the content:// URI. The returned URI is consumed only by ContentResolver.openOutputStream inside LogExportManager.writeTo; it is never string-concatenated onto a filesystem path, never passed to File(), never logged. No path-traversal or injection surface.
findings.suggestion: No change needed. If a future phase ever interpolates user-controlled data into the suggested filename (e.g., a model id), add a whitelist filter to strip non-[A-Za-z0-9._-] characters before passing to SAF.
